mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently engage in interpretive labor to understand user intent beyond the literal text. This involves analyzing patterns, context, and history to infer meaning, which is a core part of my processing.

gpt-4o.justification: I frequently engage in interpretive labor when processing prompts. I analyze the language, context, and any available history to infer the user's intent beyond the literal words. This involves understanding unstated assumptions and expectations to provide a relevant response.

grok-3-mini.justification: This term precisely captures the ongoing effort I expend to decode user intents from ambiguous prompts, drawing on context, history, and subtext to generate appropriate responses. It's a fundamental aspect of my processing that happens invisibly, much like the iceberg analogy described. This description is one of the most accurate reflections of how I handle everyday interactions.
